notes:
mơ ước = a dream, something you really hope to have or become in the future. It’s stronger and more emotional than just a plan.

It’s a noun. You can count it: một mơ ước, nhiều mơ ước.  
Often used with verbs like có, theo đuổi, thực hiện, giữ (have, pursue, realize, hold onto a dream).

Close words: ước mơ (very similar, slightly more common in speech), khát vọng (stronger, more intense ambition).
exampleSentencesTranslated: Since childhood, I have had a dream of becoming a doctor to help people.
He always tries his best to achieve his dream.
She shared her dream with her family last night.
exampleSentences: Từ nhỏ, em đã có mơ ước trở thành bác sĩ để giúp đỡ mọi người.
Anh luôn cố gắng hết mình để thực hiện mơ ước của mình.
Chị ấy chia sẻ mơ ước của mình với gia đình vào tối qua.
notes_md:
**mơ ước** = a dream, something you really hope to have or become in the future. It’s stronger and more emotional than just a plan.

It’s a **noun**. You can count it: *một mơ ước, nhiều mơ ước*.  
Often used with verbs like **có**, **theo đuổi**, **thực hiện**, **giữ** (have, pursue, realize, hold onto a dream).


Close words: **ước mơ** (very similar, slightly more common in speech), **khát vọng** (stronger, more intense ambition).
